mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate reduces discount rates and eases financial conditions, so 20-day S&P 500 returns turn positive when the policy path is down. The signal is strongest when CPIAUCSL is not accelerating, because disinflation preserves real rate relief and prevents the Fed from reversing course quickly.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher after 3-month declines in FEDFUNDS than after flat or rising FEDFUNDS, or if the effect disappears when CPIAUCSL inflation is stable to lower.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above a recent low signals weakening household income and weaker aggregate demand, which should compress earnings expectations and produce negative 20-day S&P 500 returns after the unemployment rate breaks higher. The effect should be most visible when PAYEMS also slows, confirming that labor deterioration is broad rather than a one-off survey move.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises at least 0.3pp above its 6-month low
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment breaks are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the signal has no incremental effect when PAYEMS growth is simultaneously weakening.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized improvement in GDPC1 and RSAFS indicates broad final-demand strength, which supports revenue expectations and lifts equities over the next quarter. The mechanism fails when demand gains are purely inventory-driven or when output strength is not matched by sales, because profits then do not improve sustainably.
failure_condition: Reject if positive quarterly changes in GDPC1 without concurrent RSAFS improvement do not predict higher 60-day SPX returns, or if the combined signal loses power in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO with stable or falling CPIAUCSL implies improving real activity without inflation pressure, which supports margins and multiple expansion. This should generate positive 20-day equity returns only when industrial output is not accompanied by an inflation breakout.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above its 3-month moving average while CPIAUCSL stays below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if output-strength triggers are followed by negative or flat 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal works equally well when CPIAUCSL is rising, which would contradict the low-inflation channel.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in HOUST signals a demand slowdown and tighter credit conditions ahead of weaker consumption and investment, which should weigh on equities over the next month. The effect should intensify when PAYEMS growth is also slowing, because housing weakness then reflects a broader slowdown rather than a sector-specific correction.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% below its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if large housing declines are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if PAYEMS does not materially strengthen the predictive content of the housing signal.

mechanism_narrative: A rising FEDFUNDS rate above a restrictive threshold tightens discounting and reduces risk appetite, producing negative short-horizon S&P 500 returns. The signal fails when inflation is already falling rapidly, because then higher policy rates are likely near the end of the tightening cycle and markets may look through them.
failure_condition: Reject if policy-rate levels above 4.5% are not associated with negative 5-day SPX returns, or if the effect vanishes entirely when CPIAUCSL inflation is falling.

mechanism_narrative: Strong PAYEMS growth combined with low UNRATE indicates resilient labor demand and household income growth, which supports earnings expectations and positive 60-day equity returns. This mechanism is falsified if employment gains occur while unemployment rises, because that pattern would signal noisy data rather than genuine labor-market strength.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with strong PAYEMS and low UNRATE do not produce above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the relationship reverses when UNRATE is trending upward.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in RSAFS relative to GDPC1 indicates sales are outpacing broad output, which signals improving nominal demand and near-term revenue momentum for large-cap firms. The mechanism is strongest after a negative demand shock, and it fails when GDP rises faster than sales because that implies inventory accumulation rather than end-demand strength.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth exceeds GDPC1 growth over the latest available quarter
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS-led expansions do not predict higher 60-day SPX returns, or if GDP-led periods with weaker sales perform equally well or better, which would negate the demand-led profit channel.
